message:
fix(storage): escape LIKE pattern special characters in searches

SQL LIKE patterns treat %, _, and \ as special characters. When user
input contains these characters, searches would return incorrect results
or fail silently.

Changes:
- Add escape_like_pattern() helper to escape %, _, and \ characters
- Apply escaping to title_contains filter in query_issues()
- Apply escaping to full-text search in search_issues()
- Apply escaping to title_contains filter in query_open_issues()
- Use ESCAPE '\\' clause in all LIKE expressions
- Use saturating_add() in next_child_number() to prevent overflow
- Add tracing::warn for unparseable datetime values

Example impact:
- Searching for "100%" now matches literal "100%" instead of "100" + any char
- Searching for "foo_bar" now matches literal underscore instead of any char
- Searching for paths like "C:\Users" works correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
